summary: Changed the GitHub Actions checkout configuration in .github/workflows/build.yml from fetch-depth: 1 to fetch-depth: 2. The PR was a minimal CI workflow adjustment with no discussion or review comments in the provided slice.

what_changed.change: Updated the Checkout step using actions/checkout@v3 so fetch-depth is 2 instead of 1.
what_changed.evidence: Diff shows '-        fetch-depth: 1' changed to '+        fetch-depth: 2' under the Checkout step.

smart_moves: Kept the CI change narrowly scoped to a single workflow setting, reducing risk and making intent easy to review.
Retained sparse-checkout configuration while only increasing history depth, preserving the existing optimization strategy.
decomp_lessons: CI workflow settings can affect decomp project automation even when no source files change; capture small infrastructure fixes because they may explain future build or comparison behavior.
When GitHub Actions jobs need access to more than the checked-out commit, fetch-depth: 2 is a lightweight alternative to a full clone.
follow_up_queries: Why did the build workflow need fetch-depth 2 instead of fetch-depth 1?
Which CI step in .github/workflows/build.yml depends on the previous commit or shallow history?
Are there later PRs updating actions/checkout or fetch-depth settings?
